title: Firstness Test: How Open Are You to the Undetermined?
description: Wonderer, Quick Closer, or Ambiguity Athlete? Seven questions -- no right or wrong, but a mirror of your stance toward the not-yet-determined.

questions.text: You enter a room and something catches your attention -- but you don't know what exactly. Your first impulse:

options.text: I systematically scan the room until I identify the cause.
I pause for a moment and let the feeling work before naming it.
I ask someone whether something is different from usual.
I enjoy the irritation and observe how my perception slowly sharpens.

questions.text: AI delivers a perfect answer in seconds to a question that has occupied you for a long time. Your feeling:

options.text: Relief -- finally clarity. On to the next problem.
Skepticism -- no good answer can emerge that quickly.
Grief -- the question was more valuable to me than any answer.
Curiosity -- I question the answer and let the question continue to work.

questions.text: A discussion gets heated. Both sides have strong arguments. You notice you don't have a clear position. What do you do?

options.text: I pick a side -- ambivalence is weakness.
I formulate a compromise that considers both sides.
I hold the tension and try to understand the contradiction more deeply.
I ask a question that irritates both sides.

questions.text: You face a blank page. No assignment, no deadline, no guidelines. What happens?

options.text: I immediately find myself a task or template -- emptiness is unproductive.
I ask ChatGPT for ideas to find a starting point.
I let the emptiness stand for a while. Sometimes something emerges from nothing.
I begin with a line, a word, a sound -- without knowing where it leads.

questions.text: What does 'learning' mean to you at its core?

options.text: Acquiring knowledge and being able to apply it.
Recognizing patterns and transferring them.
Being irritated and developing new questions from that.
Changing without knowing beforehand where it leads.

questions.text: A colleague shows you a piece of work you can't categorize. It's neither good nor bad, just... different. Your reaction:

options.text: I ask for an explanation -- I can't evaluate without context.
I compare it to things I know to find a frame of reference.
I look at it for a long time and wait until something resonates within me.
I'm fascinated. The uncategorizable is precisely what interests me.

questions.text: The decisive competence of the future is, for you:

options.text: Efficiency -- whoever uses AI best, wins.
Adaptability -- responding flexibly to change.
Judgment -- discerning where AI ends and being human begins.
Wonder -- the ability to notice the unexpected before it is named.

staunen.title: Wonderer -- Firstness as a Stance
staunen.description: You live in Firstness. You notice what others overlook, ask before others answer, and find the unnamed more fascinating than the explained. In the AI age, you are the source of genuine novelty -- the machine can do many things, but it cannot wonder.

verweilen.title: Ambiguity Athlete -- At Home in the Undetermined
verweilen.description: You hold tension where others close quickly. You stay in contradiction, let emptiness become productive, and trust that insight needs time. Your strength: you resist the temptation of the quick answer.
verweilen.motto: Dwell in the undetermined without prematurely closing it.

schliessen.title: Quick Closer -- Thirdness as Reflex
schliessen.description: You seek clarity, structure, answers. That's not wrong -- but in the AI age, the machine takes over exactly this work. The Atelier invites you to slow down: the right question is worth more than the quick answer. Firstness awaits you.
schliessen.motto: The quickest answer is rarely the deepest.
